title: Link preview cards: OG metadata fetch, cache, serve
description: When a post contains a URL, fetch the page's OpenGraph/Twitter Card metadata (title, description, image, site_name). Cache the result. Serve via the Mastodon card API field on status objects. Requirements: SSRF-safe fetching (reuse existing guards), async background fetch so posting isn't blocked, image proxying/caching for the preview image, timeout + size limits on fetches, graceful degradation (no card if fetch fails). Clients like Phanpy/Ivory show these prominently — high user-visible value.
design: 1. New cards table: post_id, url, title, description, image_url, image_cached_path, site_name, fetched_at. 2. On post creation, extract first http/https URL from content. 3. Spawn background task to fetch URL, parse OG tags, download+resize preview image. 4. Serve card data in outbox Note objects as attachment or via future Mastodon API. 5. SSRF: reuse is_private_host_resolved + read_body_limited (cap HTML fetch to 1MB, image to 5MB). 6. Parse with a lightweight HTML meta extractor (no full DOM). 7. Cache cards permanently (URLs don't change metadata often).
